body.title: [sprint] 67 files SELF-APPOINT as 'the canonical source'. Canonicity must be GRANTED by an intention/decision — fold the check into validity_sweep.
body.description:
Akien, 2026-07-13: 'any file that's more than a month old that says it's the canonical source of anything should be checked against intentions.'

MEASURED: 67 files in the tree ASSERT canonicity ('canonical source' / 'single source' / 'source of truth' / 'authoritative' / 'master copy'). Exactly 1 INTENTION and 19 decisions even MENTION canonicity — and most of those name no file. Two of the asserting files are 50 days old.

SO: canonicity is currently SELF-APPOINTED. Nothing grants it, and nothing checks it.

THE SHARPENING (CC.0, and it is what makes the check runnable): a file that declares 'I am the SINGLE canonical source' with no grant behind it is self-appointed authority — STRUCTURALLY IDENTICAL TO A CONFABULATION. A confident claim, no backing, believed by future readers PRECISELY BECAUSE it sounds authoritative.

RULE (R-canonicity-is-granted-not-asserted): a file MAY NOT declare itself canonical. Canonicity is a GRANT, recorded in an intention or decision that NAMES the file. A file may only POINT AT its grant.

WHY THAT MATTERS PRACTICALLY: it converts an expensive judgment ('is this claim still true?' — needs a human, so nobody ever does it) into a mechanical grep ('does an intention/decision NAME this path?'). Age becomes the TRIGGER FOR REVIEW, not the evidence of rot.

IT HAS ALREADY COST US, four times in one day (2026-07-12):
  - skills/questions/questions.json says 'This is the SINGLE canonical source'. Written 2026-06-25; wrong within 3 weeks; it fooled CC.0, who built a second home for the cognition corpus before finding it.
  - A note titled 'MILESTONE — ... MEASURED at 51% token reduction' (it was an ESTIMATE) made CC.0 argue 51% at Akien while holding the correct 89.19% in memory. A stale headline beat a correct memory.
  - identity.home_db_url() and db_proxy.make_home_proxy() make OPPOSITE authoritative claims about the SAME env var — and the employer-privacy rule rests on that boundary.
  - 8 skills run with no repo source, so every skills audit was scoped to a tree that did not contain the broken ones.

IMPLEMENTATION — FOLD INTO validity_sweep.py. Do NOT build a new tool (homogeneity over special-case; it already runs at day-close, already flags stale entries, already prints flagged/checked/unresolvable). Add a canonicity pass:
  1. Grep the tree for canonicity assertions (the phrase set above).
  2. For each, look for a GRANT: an intention or decision naming that path.
  3. No grant, OR the asserting file is newer than its grant, OR the assertion is >30d old and unreviewed -> FLAG. Annotate, never delete (CP2).
  4. Print `canonicity: asserted=N granted=M ungranted=K` alongside the existing sweep line.

THEN: work the ungranted list. Each one either EARNS a grant (an intention names it) or LOSES the claim (strip the words).

**Test plan:** red->green: validity_sweep reports the ungranted-canonicity count; a fixture file asserting canonicity with no grant is FLAGGED; one with a grant is not. Guard test: no NEW file may assert canonicity without a grant.

**Decision:** rule:R-canonicity-is-granted-not-asserted

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
